messages.content: hey gpt can you help me create some comprensive list on a particula subject
Of course! I'd be happy to help you create a comprehensive list on a particular subject. Please let me know which subject you'd like me to focus on, and I'll do my best to assist you.
i wouls like a list of all dnd source materials your familiar  with starting at 1sr edition up to 5e along with the year of publications

Certainly! Here's a comprehensive list of Dungeons & Dragons source materials starting from 1st edition up to 5th edition, including their year of publication:

1st Edition:
1. Dungeons & Dragons (1974) - Original box set
2. Advanced Dungeons & Dragons Player's Handbook (1978)
3. Advanced Dungeons & Dragons Monster Manual (1977)
4. Advanced Dungeons & Dragons Dungeon Master's Guide (1979)
5. Deities & Demigods (1980)
6. Fiend Folio (1981)
7. Unearthed Arcana (1985)
8. Oriental Adventures (1985)
9. Dungeoneer's Survival Guide (1986)
10. Wilderness Survival Guide (1986)
11. Manual of the Planes (1987)

2nd Edition:
1. Player's Handbook (1989)
2. Dungeon Master Guide (1989)
3. Monstrous Compendium (1989)
4. Spelljammer: Adventures in Space (1989)
5. Al-Qadim: Arabian Adventures (1992)
6. Forgotten Realms Campaign Setting (1993)
7. Dragonlance Campaign Setting (1993)
8. Ravenloft Campaign Setting (1994)
9. Dark Sun Campaign Setting (1991)
10. Birthright Campaign Setting (1995)

3rd Edition:
1. Player's Handbook (2000)
2. Dungeon Master's Guide (2000)
3. Monster Manual (2000)
4. Sword and Fist (2001)
5. Defenders of the Faith (2001)
6. Book of Vile Darkness (2002)
7. Tome and Blood (2001)
8. Expanded Psionics Handbook (2004)

3.5 Edition:
1. Player's Handbook (2003)
2. Dungeon Master's Guide (2003)
3. Monster Manual (2003)
4. Complete Arcane (2004)
5. Complete Divine (2004)
6. Complete Warrior (2003)
7. Races of Stone (2004)
8. Fiendish Codex I: Hordes of the Abyss (2006)

4th Edition:
1. Player's Handbook (2008)
2. Dungeon Master's Guide (2008)
3. Monster Manual (2008)
4. Player's Handbook 2 (2009)
5. Monster Manual 2 (2009)
6. Dungeon Master's Guide 2 (2009)
7. Player's Handbook 3 (2010)
8. Monster Manual 3 (2010)
9. Dungeon Master's Guide 3 (2010)

5th Edition:
1. Player's Handbook (2014)
2. Dungeon Master's Guide (2014)
3. Monster Manual (2014)
4. Xanathar's Guide to Everything (2017)
5. Mordenkainen's Tome of Foes (2018)
6. Volo's Guide to Monsters (2016)
7. Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide (2015)

Please note that this is not an exhaustive list, as there are numerous additional supplements, adventures, and campaign settings released for each edition.

ok can you make list for all the races however to reduce token fomat to # race [1e 2e) though the contents of the []would be alter to include1e-5e for which editions it were in note that if present in all the []can simple read [all[

Certainly! Here is a list of races in Dungeons & Dragons, noting in which editions they were present:

1. Dwarf [1e-5e]
2. Elf [1e-5e]
3. Gnome [1e-5e]
4. Halfling [1e-5e]
5. Half-Elf [1e-5e]
6. Half-Orc [1e-5e]
7. Human [1e-5e]
8. Dragonborn [4e-5e]
9. Tiefling [4e-5e]
10. Aasimar [2e-5e]
11. Eladrin [4e-5e]
12. Warforged [3.5e, 4e, 5e]
13. Genasi [4e-5e]
14. Goliath [3.5e, 4e, 5e]
15. Shifters [4e-5e]
16. Changelings [4e, 5e]
17. Kalashtar [4e, 5e]
18. Minotaur [4e, 5e]
19. Shardmind [4e]
20. Deva [4e]
21. Githyanki [1e-5e]
22. Githzerai [1e-5e]
23. Kobold [1e-5e]
24. Orc [1e-5e]
25. Tabaxi [5e]
26. Triton [5e]
27. Yuan-Ti Pureblood [5e]
28. Kenku [5e]
29. Lizardfolk [5e]
30. Firbolg [5e]
31. Aarakocra [5e]
32. Tortle [5e]
33. Gnome (Deep/Svirfneblin) [2e-5e]
34. Duergar [3.5e, 5e]
35. Aasimar (Fallen) [5e]
36. Aasimar (Scourge) [5e]
37. Aasimar (Protector) [5e]
38. Hobgoblin [5e]
39. Bugbear [5e]
40. Goblin [5e]
41. Kobold (Wingless) [5e]

Note: The races listed above are not exclusive, and there may be additional variant races or homebrew options available in different campaign settings or supplements for each edition.
can you do the same for classes and sub classes

Absolutely! Here's a list of classes and subclasses in Dungeons & Dragons, noting in which editions they were present:

1. Barbarian [2e-5e]
2. Bard [2e-5e]
3. Cleric [1e-5e]
    - Subclasses: Domain-specific variants introduced after 3rd edition
4. Druid [1e-5e]
    - Subclasses: Circle of the Land, Circle of the Moon, introduced after 3rd edition
5. Fighter [1e-5e]
    - Subclasses: Battle Master, Champion, Eldritch Knight, introduced after 3rd edition
6. Monk [2e-5e]
    - Subclasses: Way of the Open Hand, Way of Shadow, Way of the Four Elements, introduced after 3rd edition
7. Paladin [1e-5e]
    - Subclasses: Oath-specific variants introduced after 3rd edition
8. Ranger [1e-5e]
    - Subclasses: Hunter, Beast Master, introduced after 3rd edition
9. Rogue [2e-5e]
    - Subclasses: Thief, Assassin, Arcane Trickster, introduced after 3rd edition
10. Sorcerer [3e-5e]
    - Subclasses: Draconic Bloodline, Wild Magic, introduced in 3rd edition
11. Warlock [3.5e-5e]
    - Subclasses: The Archfey, The Fiend, The Great Old One, introduced in 3.5e
12. Wizard [1e-5e]
    - Subclasses: School of Abjuration, School of Conjuration, School of Divination, School of Enchantment, School of Evocation, School of Illusion, School of Necromancy, School of Transmutation, introduced after 3rd edition

Note: The list above includes the core classes and their major subclasses. Additional variant classes or homebrew options may exist in different campaign settings or supplements for each edition.
do you understand contenet of tribal when aplly to mgt
I apologize, but I don't understand what you mean by "mgt" in this context. Could you please clarify or provide more information?

No problem! In the context of "mtg," it refers to Magic: The Gathering, a popular collectible card game. As for the term "tribal," in Magic: The Gathering, it is used to describe a specific card type or deck archetype that focuses on creature types, such as goblins, elves, vampires, etc. Tribal decks typically include cards that synergize with specific creature types to maximize their effectiveness. These decks often feature tribal-themed mechanics and abilities that enhance the creatures of a particular type.
grat can we make a list simimlar to thos above for the mtg tribal creature types

Certainly! Here's a list of some popular tribal creature types in Magic: The Gathering:

1. Goblin
2. Elf
3. Vampire
4. Wizard
5. Zombie
6. Merfolk
7. Human
8. Dragon
9. Angel
10. Faerie
11. Spirit
12. Soldier
13. Elemental
14. Beast
15. Shaman
16. Knight
17. Dwarf
18. Giant
19. Goblin Shaman
20. Wizard Zombie
21. Sliver
22. Goblin Rogue
23. Treefolk
24. Elemental Incarnation

Please note that this is not an exhaustive list, as Magic: The Gathering has a vast number of creature types available for tribal decks, and new ones are frequently introduced with each set. Additionally, some creature types may have equivalent variations or subtypes, so the list provided encompasses commonly recognized tribal creature types.

if i asked you to restart or refresh instance could you

As an AI language model, I don't have the capability to restart or refresh my own instance. However, if you have any questions or need assistance with something specific, feel free to ask, and I'll do my best to assist you!
